message.content:
I need to understand the patterns used in existing screen implementations for this Raycast UI clone project. Please read and summarize the key patterns from these files:

1. src/data/snippet_data.ts - data structure patterns
2. src/data/file_search_data.tsx - data structure with icons
3. src/components/snippet_manager/snippet_manager_view.tsx - full screen view pattern (how it uses SearchBar, List, ActionPanel, Alert, HUD)
4. src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx - another view pattern
5. src/registry/commands.ts - how commands are registered
6. src/types.ts - shared types

Focus on:
- Data type definitions and structure
- How views are structured (SearchBar + List + ActionPanel integration)
- Self-contained view pattern (isFullView)
- How actions/keyboard shortcuts are wired
- How commands are registered in the registry
- Import patterns and barrel exports

Working directory: /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol

content.text: I'll analyze these key files to understand the patterns used in this Raycast UI clone project. Let me start by reading all the files in parallel.

1→import type { TagColor } from '../constants/tag_colors'; 2→ 3→export interface SnippetEntry { 4→ id: string; 5→ name: string; 6→ keyword: string; 7→ content: string; 8→ category: 'general' | 'code' | 'email' | 'template'; 9→ tags: { 10→ text: string; 11→ color?: TagColor; 12→ }[]; 13→ createdAt: Date; 14→} 15→ 16→export type SnippetCategory = SnippetEntry['category']; 17→ 18→const SNIPPET_CATEGORY_VALUES: SnippetCategory[] = [ 19→ 'general', 20→ 'code', 21→ 'email', 22→ 'template', 23→]; 24→ 25→export function isSnippetCategory(v: string): v is SnippetCategory { 26→ return (SNIPPET_CATEGORY_VALUES as string[]).includes(v); 27→} 28→ 29→const now = Date.now(); 30→ 31→export const SNIPPET_CATEGORIES = [ 32→ { 33→ label: 'General', 34→ value: 'general', 35→ }, 36→ { 37→ label: 'Code', 38→ value: 'code', 39→ }, 40→ { 41→ label: 'Email', 42→ value: 'email', 43→ }, 44→ { 45→ label: 'Template', 46→ value: 'template', 47→ }, 48→] as const; 49→ 50→export const SNIPPET_TAGS = [ 51→ { 52→ label: 'JavaScript', 53→ value: 'javascript', 54→ color: 'yellow' as TagColor, 55→ }, 56→ { 57→ label: 'TypeScript', 58→ value: 'typescript', 59→ color: 'blue' as TagColor, 60→ }, 61→ { 62→ label: 'Python', 63→ value: 'python', 64→ color: 'green' as TagColor, 65→ }, 66→ { 67→ label: 'React', 68→ value: 'react', 69→ color: 'blue' as TagColor, 70→ }, 71→ { 72→ label: 'CSS', 73→ value: 'css', 74→ color: 'purple' as TagColor, 75→ }, 76→ { 77→ label: 'HTML', 78→ value: 'html', 79→ color: 'orange' as TagColor, 80→ }, 81→ { 82→ label: 'Shell', 83→ value: 'shell', 84→ color: 'green' as TagColor, 85→ }, 86→ { 87→ label: 'Markdown', 88→ value: 'markdown', 89→ color: 'orange' as TagColor, 90→ }, 91→] as const;    255→export function getFileIcon(fileType: FileType): ReactNode {
   256→  switch (fileType) {
   257→    case 'folder':
   258→      return <FileFolderIcon />;
   259→    case 'code':
   260→      return <FileCodeIcon />;
   261→    case 'image':
   262→      return <FileImageIcon />;
   263→    case 'pdf':
   264→      return <FilePdfIcon />;
   265→    case 'archive':
   266→      return <FileArchiveIcon />;
   267→    case 'spreadsheet':
   268→      return <FileSpreadsheetIcon />;
   269→    case 'presentation':
   270→      return <FilePresentationIcon />;
   271→    case 'document':
   272→      return <FileDocumentIcon />;
   273→  }
   274→}
